meaning: n.装订,捆绑,捆绑物,粘合物
examples: It is set out in a legally binding protocol which forms part of the treaty.
Clayton sought to examine evidence of scrub jays' accurate memory of "what," "where," and "when" information and their binding of this information.
She hurriedly untied the ropes binding her ankles.
Some fertility experts fear the guidelines may not lead to changes because they are not binding.
There's no legal - you know, legally binding aspect of - it's just to honor her wish.
